findings.reachability: Any authenticated WordPress user who can update their own profile display_name (subscriber and above by default) and whose display_name renders in the admin notice visible to other admins on the plugin settings page.
findings.justification: $current_user->display_name is concatenated directly into HTML markup without esc_html() or any escaping before being passed to printf(), so a user-controlled display_name containing HTML/script tags will be rendered verbatim in the admin notice.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: unescaped display_name in HTML output
detection_pattern.description: Catches concatenation or direct use of $current_user->display_name (or similar user-profile fields) into HTML strings without context-correct escaping. WordPress does not sanitize display_name for HTML on save, so it can contain tags that execute when echoed into page output.
